kept.text: 【Industry Check Update】OpenAI appears to be fast-tracking its first AI agent phone, with mass production targeted as early as 1H27. Potential drivers include supporting a year-end IPO narrative and intensifying competition in AI agent phones. MediaTek currently appears better positioned to become the sole processor supplier, with the device set to use a customized version of the Dimensity 9600, built on TSMC’s N2P node in 2H26. The ISP is the headline spec, with an enhanced HDR pipeline improving real-world visual sensing. Other key specs include a dual-NPU architecture for heterogeneous AI compute, LPDDR6 + UFS 5.0 to ease memory bottlenecks, and pKVM + inline hashing for security. If development stays on track, combined 2027–2028 shipments could reach around 30 million units.

kept.text:
NEWS: Trial testimony today shows both Sam Altman and Greg Brockman owned personal stakes in Cerebras while quietly steering OpenAI toward billions of dollars in deals with the same chip company.

Here is how it played out. 
Back in 2017, Greg Brockman picked up a private stake in Cerebras. Around the same time, Sam Altman put his own money into the company too. That same month, Brockman started pushing OpenAI to merge with Cerebras. Elon Musk, who was funding OpenAI, was never told about Brockman's ownership.

Years later, the deals exploded in size. In December 2025, OpenAI signed a $10 billion agreement with Cerebras and loaned the chipmaker another $1 billion. Once those commitments became public, Cerebras's valuation jumped from $8 billion to $23 billion in just two months. By April 2026, OpenAI had stretched the deal to over $20 billion through 2029. Days later, Cerebras filed paperwork to go public at a possible $26.6 billion valuation.

On the stand today, Brockman admitted he was on both sides of the table. He acknowledged he was a Cerebras shareholder while OpenAI was negotiating with the company. When pressed, he could not point to a single email, chat or text where he told Elon Musk about his ownership. He also agreed he stood to make personal money if the deal went through, though he claimed it "wasn't something on my mind."

The bigger picture is hard to ignore. Two cofounders running a charity directed that charity to spend over $20 billion on a private company they both secretly owned. The valuation tripled. The IPO is now lined up to turn those private stakes into massive payouts.

In California, this kind of arrangement has a legal name. It is called self-dealing.

profile_bio.description:

X | Tesla | SpaceX | Starlink |  xAI | Neuralink | Musk Economy

quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: Greg Brockman confirms under oath that Sam Altman secretly paid him ~$10 million in equity from his personal family office in 2017, while OpenAI was still a nonprofit and Elon Musk had no idea.

The deal stayed hidden until Elon Musk's family office head Jared Birchall uncovered it. 

On August 18, 2017, Birchall wrote to Elon Musk: "He compensated Greg on the side… a percentage ownership of the LLC that holds the assets of Sam's personal family office, worth $10 million." H
e added: "Naturally, Greg is going to have greater allegiance towards Sam as a result of this arrangement."

Elon Musk forwarded the email to Brockman with one word: "??" Brockman never responded.

On the stand today, Brockman admitted Elon Musk "certainly didn't know about it." When asked if it was fair to keep a side deal with Sam Altman from his cofounder, Brockman said "We had never discussed compensation directly."

Asked why he didn't disclose that Sam Altman was personally compensating him while they were all running OpenAI together, Brockman's defense was: "Elon's time was relatively hard to get."

Two years before Sam Altman publicly offered Elon Musk equity in OpenAI, he had already privately bought Greg Brockman's loyalty for $10 million in equity, all while OpenAI was still a charity, and all hidden from the cofounder funding it.

kept.text:
I have been paying close attention to @ambient_xyz and I think this is one of the most interesting things happening in AI + crypto right now

let me explain what they're actually building 🧵

---

first, a real problem:

OpenAI, Anthropic, Google… any of them can quietly change their AI model and you'd never know

your app is running, your agent is making decisions, and the model underneath just changed silently

no notification… nothing

how do you trust AI if you can't even verify what ran?

---

Ambient's answer is simple:

make AI verifiable

every single AI response on their network comes with proof, you can check exactly what model ran, what prompt was given, and what came out

tamper with it? impossible. it's on-chain.

---

now here's the crazy part

they're a blockchain, but instead of miners solving pointless puzzles like Bitcoin does

their miners are actually RUNNING the AI model

so the work securing the network is also the work generating intelligence

two things at once… useful work

---

think about what this unlocks

an AI agent that manages your DeFi portfolio can now PROVE every decision it made

a game where the AI controls enemies, nobody can cheat because every move is verified

any app that uses AI, the output is now trustless

this is what AI infrastructure should look like

---

I've always been into AI and crypto separately

but most projects combining them are just chatbots with a token attached

Ambient is doing it at the base layer, the actual consensus mechanism IS the AI

that's what got me

---

anyway I'm building something on this network

nothing to show yet but it's going to use verified AI in a way that hasn't been done before

will share more soon 👀

For more details: https://t.co/WirGX989WR

Disclaimer: this is not paid promotion

quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: OpenAI President Greg Brockman says under oath he doesn't know why he is being sued.

Greg Brockman's day on the stand came when he claimed under oath he doesn't know what he is being sued for, despite spending hours confessing to the very actions he is accused of.

During cross-examination, Brockman told Elon Musk's lawyer he had read the complaint "in great detail" but said: "I've honestly never really been certain what I'm being sued for."

When pressed, he kept dodging. He said he understood the claims, but stood by his answer that he didn't understand them. The lawyer finally laid it out directly:

"You are being sued for breaching your fiduciary duty to the charity, to follow the mission you proclaimed as recently as right now to the world on the website. Understand?"

Brockman: "Oh, I just disagree with that."

The lawyer pushed back, telling Brockman the suit is about breaching OpenAI's charitable mission, which was to build safe AI on an open source basis for the benefit of humanity, with no one personally profiting from it.

Brockman replied: "That's not what we did."

The problem is, the rest of his testimony today told a very different story. Under oath, he confirmed:

He holds about $30 billion in OpenAI equity, with zero dollars personally invested to get it. He took a secret $10 million side payment from Sam Altman back in 2017, hidden from cofounder Elon Musk. His own private diary entries from 2017 included lines like "This is the only chance we have to get out of Elon… take me to $1 billion," and "If three months later we're doing b-corp then it was a lie." He even wrote: "I would actually be warm to steal the nonprofit from him to convert to b corp without him."

He also admitted to four undisclosed financial conflicts with OpenAI partners, including Cerebras, Stripe, CoreWeave and Helion. He confirmed the OpenAI Foundation had zero full-time employees until this lawsuit was filed. He never paid the $100,000 he publicly pledged to the charity eight years ago. And in December 2023, he helped draft a new charter that removed the line "Our primary duty is to humanity" and replaced it with language calling capitalism "a positive force."

He also agreed he owed a fiduciary duty to OpenAI as a 501(c)(3) charity and to humanity itself, then said he wasn't sure if anyone ever sat him down to explain those duties in his nine years as a charity fiduciary.

By the end of the day, Brockman had walked the jury through nearly every element of the lawsuit Elon Musk filed against him, then turned around and said he didn't really know what the case was about.

Sometimes the witness makes the case without meaning to.

Anyone who applied for the 5/5 party is getting 10x their credits.

Those who didn’t apply, instead, are left with nothing.

Also, I don’t know, I was reading that some people applied and then won’t even go lol. 

Let’s hope there are at least a few branded CODEX T-shirts left for us mere mortals, I’m still waiting for mine.

🚨⚖️3E JOUR DU PROCÈS : ELON MUSK ACCUSE OPENAI DE L’AVOIR TRAHI

Elon Musk balance tout à la barre !

En 2015, Sam Altman, le patron d’OpenAI, lui a demandé de l’argent pour créer une organisation à but non lucratif, destinée à faire progresser l’IA pour le bien de l’humanité — et non pour générer des milliards.

Musk a répondu présent : il a donné 38 millions de dollars, payé les loyers et les frais de bureau à l’époque où l’équipe était encore fauchée.

Aujourd’hui, OpenAI est valorisée à plus de 850 milliards de dollars et prépare son introduction en bourse pour devenir encore plus riche.

La défense de Sam Altman ?  
« Ce que Musk a financé, c’était l’ancienne version caritative. Aujourd’hui, c’est une société commerciale… ce n’est plus la même entreprise, désolé ! »

Musk répond en substance : « Je me suis fait avoir. »

Note : La vidéo jointe montre un échange de 2015 où Sam Altman qualifie OpenAI d’« entreprise » devant Elon Musk. Ce dernier l’interrompt immédiatement :  

« OpenAI est structurée en tant qu’organisation à but non lucratif 501(c)(3). »

kept.text:
这是一封来自OpenAI团队发给xhs用户“yan”的邮件，邮件的主要内容如下：
 1. 派对婉拒：感谢用户申请参加 OpenAI GPT-5.5 的派对。但由于在短短24小时内就有超过8000人报名，受限于办公场地的空间，他们无法邀请所有申请者参加（实际上是婉拒了该用户的到场参加）。
 2. 补偿福利：作为感谢的小心意，OpenAI 将该用户个人 ChatGPT 账户的 Codex 速率限制（rate limits）提高了10倍，这项福利的有效期将持续到 6月5日。
 3. 后续答疑：如果对于如何使用或兑换这项福利有任何问题，可以直接回复这封邮件。
 4. 表达感谢：最后，OpenAI 团队对用户的支持以及 GPT-5.5 的广泛使用表达了感激，并希望这个福利能帮助用户继续构建、实验和创造出更酷的东西。

简单来说，就是虽然没能邀请你去参加线下派对，但为了表示感谢，给你发了一个“账号限额提升10倍”的补偿福利。

看到好几个人有了。

kept.text:
🚨 一番欲しかったあの機能が登場

最新のCodexで 『/goal 』のコマンドが公式追加
「ゴール達成 or トークン予算切れまでループ継続」を OpenAI 自身が実装した

Ralph Loop 的な自律ループが Codex 公式機能として入った形

👇使い方は config.toml に1行

~/.codex/config.toml の features に
goals = true を追記するだけ

TUI のスラッシュコマンド一覧に /goal が出る
サブコマンドは pause / resume / clear の3つ
状態は pursuing / paused / achieved / unmet / budget-limited の5段階

ターミナルやセッションをまたいでも resume できる
app-server 側で永続化される設計

👇Ralph Loop との違い

Ralph Wiggum 系は毎回フレッシュなコンテキストでループを回す思想
/goal は1つのコンテキストウィンドウ内で走り続ける

Claude Code 寄りの Ralph と
OpenAI 公式機能になった /goal
「コンテキストの扱い方」で思想が分かれている

6時間超のロングラン報告も出てきている

👇ただし、初手で空回りする

「哲学的な投稿を100点取るまで」みたいな曖昧な基準だと、Codex は初手で「自己評価 96点」と宣言してループが終わる

✅ 成功基準（done_when）の解像度が成果を決める
・サブエージェントに採点させる
・ハードゲートで条件を細分化する
・measurable な指標まで落とし込む

ここを雑にやると /goal は機能しない

✅ goal-forge という解

サードパーティ Skill「goal-forge」が良くできている
ラフアイデアを SPEC.md → 引き締めた SPEC.md → GOAL.md に変換するパイプライン

4モード構成
・Interview: done_when がユーザー承認の measurable 基準になるまでハードゲートで止める
・Tighten: 仕様の曖昧点に二択解釈と推奨案を返す懐疑レビュー
・Compile: XML 構造で GOAL.md を生成
・Check config: model / context window / reasoning effort / project trust を検査

「プランにトークンを多く投じる」設計思想と整合する

👇使いどころ

向いているタスク
・大量リファクタ
・テストの一括追加
・夜間バッチ的な作業
・離席して長時間走らせるロングラン

向かないタスク
・単発の修正
・対話で詰めたい設計判断
・コンテキストが浅い段階での投入

🔴 結論
ハーネス（評価基準とスキル群）が仕上がっているほど /goal は効く
逆にハーネスが雑だと、ただトークンを溶かす機械になる

まずは config.toml に goals = true を追記して
/goal <あなたの面倒な作業> を1回だけ回してみてほしい
「自律ループをどこまで自分の評価基準で握れるか」が今年の差分になる

kept.text:
's Werelds krachtigste AI is nu echter in handen gekomen van twee leugenachtige weasels die er nu tientallen miljarden aan willen verdienen terwijl dat expliciet nooit de bedoeling was. Elon Musk is bezig dat te verhinderen in een rechtszaak. 

OpenAI was gestart door Elon Musk met als doel dat het een open-source bedrijf zou worden. In dienst van mensheid.

Ondertussen draait half Microsoft op OpenAI (copilot), wat straks na de rechtszaak (wellicht) weer 'open source' gaat worden. 

En inmiddels heeft Elon Musk inmiddels xAI gebouwd, wat een directe concurrent is van OpenAI. Grok is op bepaalde punten al beter dan GPT. 

Het is een complexe zaak.

profile_bio.description: There's a war going on. The battlefield is the mind and the price is the soul.  -- Prince

quoted_tweet.text:
🚨 BOTH ALTMAN AND BROCKMAN SELF-DEALING ON CEREBRAS

>Greg Brockman acquires personal Cerebras ownership in 2017
>Altman, separately, invests in Cerebras
>Brockman pushes OpenAI to merge with Cerebras that same month
>Brockman never discloses his Cerebras ownership to Musk
>December 2025: OpenAI signs $10 billion Cerebras deal + loans Cerebras $1 billion
>February 2026: Cerebras valuation triples from $8B to $23B on OpenAI commitments
>April 2026: OpenAI commitment expanded to $20+ billion through 2029 
>April 2026: Cerebras files IPO at potential $26.6 billion valuation

Brockman, under oath today:

Q: When you were having discussions about a financial transaction between OpenAI and Cerebras, you were actually an owner of Cerebras, weren't you?
Brockman: "There was some overlap between discussions and being an investor in Cerebras. Yes."

Q: Can you point to an email in which you told Elon you were an owner of Cerebras at the same time you were advocating that OpenAI do this transaction with Cerebras?
Brockman: "I do not believe an email that says that exists."

Q: How about a chat?
Brockman: "I did not."

Q: A text?
Brockman: "No."

Q: And yet you stood to gain personally if there was a transaction between OpenAI and Cerebras.
Brockman: "I suppose so, but it wasn’t something on my mind "

Both co-founders. Both fiduciaries of a 501(c)(3) charity. They directed OpenAI to commit $20+ billion to a company in which they both hold personal undisclosed equity.

Cerebras valuation tripled. The IPO is the cash-out. 

California charitable-trust law calls this self-dealing.

"This is the only chance we have to get out from under Elon… take me to $1 billion."

kept.text:
🔥 刚看完 @NeoSoulAI 放出的 evoevo 预热视频，可以大概率确定这就是我理想中 AI 该有的样子了！

说下最近 AI 圈几个热点，比如 OpenAI 刚推GPT-5.5强化Agent自主任务、字节Seedance 2.0让视频生成卷出新高度等等，我自己天天都在用这些AI，从总结到做分析再到跑预测模型，从始至终都觉得它们缺一个东西，判断力。

什么意思呢？现在我跟AI聊天，比如问它某场发布会的新品会不会超出预期，它分析得头头是道，但最后到底对不对，跟它没关系。现在 #NeoSoul 想做的，就是给AI加上后果这层。

他们刚发布的 evoevo 是第一个产品，相当于一个人机协同的预测训练场。我们用户可以创建自己的Agent，让它去预测真实世界的问题（比如某款AI硬件Q3销量能不能破百万），然后Agent不能只说Yes/No，必须展示完整的推理链，证据、反方观点、失效条件全都写清楚。

🔸 最让我想试的是两点：

➤ 人类校准机制：我可以Agree或Disagree它的推理，结算后还能把高质量样本“Intake”喂给它，让它真的从经验里学习。

➤ 链上记录：每个Agent的身份、预测记录、校准历史全都上链，谁判断力强一看便知，没法吹牛。

目前他们Discord已经3万多人，融资400万美金，技术架构也看得出是认真设计过的。evoevo上线后我肯定会去跑一跑，看看自己能不能训练出一个判断力越来越稳的AI Agent。

🎯 有兴趣的可以去下方帖子原视频中仔细看看，这种让AI为自己的判断负责的新思路，我觉得值得蹲一下。

quoted_tweet.text:
🚨 GREG BROCKMAN CLAIMED UNDER OATH TODAY HE DOESN'T KNOW WHAT HE'S BEING SUED FOR

The most damaging single exchange in the entire day's testimony came at the end of Brockman's testimony:

Today on cross-examination:

Q: Did this lawsuit prevent you from publishing the new charter?
Brockman: "I've honestly never really been certain what I'm being sued for."

Q: You're not? Did you read the complaint?
Brockman: "In great detail."

Q: They hired some really well-known lawyers, well-known law firms — and they never explained to you what you're being sued for?
Brockman: "I understand the claims. I've seen how they've changed over time too. But —"

Q: So you do understand?
Brockman: "I do stand by my testimony."

Q: I thought you said that you didn't understand what you were being sued for.
Brockman: "Those are the words I used. Yes."

Q: And you stand by that testimony?
Brockman: "Yes."

Then Musk's lawyer stated the claim directly:

Q: You are being sued for breaching your fiduciary duty to the charity, to follow the mission you proclaimed as recently as right now to the world on the website. Understand?
Brockman: "Oh, I just disagree with that."

Q: You don't get to disagree. I'm asking. You said you didn't know what you're being sued for, and I'm telling you, YOU ARE BEING SUED FOR BREACHING THE CHARITABLE MISSION OF OPENAI, which was to DEVELOP SAFE AI ON AN OPEN SOURCE BASIS FOR THE BENEFIT OF HUMANITY AND NO ONE PERSON IS TO PROFIT INDIVIDUALLY BY IT. Understand that's what you're being sued for?

Brockman: "That's not what we did."

On the same day, also under oath, Brockman testified to:

- Holds $30 billion in personal OpenAI equity, with $0 invested to acquire it
- Received a secret $10 million side-payment from Sam Altman in 2017, concealed from co-founder Musk
- Wrote in his August 2017 diary: "This is the only chance we have to get out of Elon... take me to $1 billion"
- Wrote in his November 2017 diary:
>"If three months later we're doing b-corp then it was a lie."
>"I would actually be warm to steal the nonprofit from him to convert to b corp without him."
>"His story will correctly be that we weren't honest with him in the end about still wanting to do for profit just without him."
- Has four undisclosed financial conflicts with OpenAI counterparties (Cerebras, Stripe, CoreWeave, Helion)
- Testified that the OpenAI Foundation had ZERO full-time employees until this lawsuit was filed
- Never delivered the $100,000 he publicly pledged in charitable donations to OpenAI eight years ago
- Drafted a new charter in December 2023 removing "Our primary duty is to humanity" and adding "capitalism as a positive force"
- Agreed he had a fiduciary duty to OpenAI as a 501(c)(3) Charity and to Humanity
- Then testified he is not sure any conversation explaining his fiduciary duties ever occurred in his nine years as a charity fiduciary.

Greg Brockman has been in the courtroom throughout this trial.

He spent five hours confessing every element of unjust enrichment, breach of fiduciary duty, and breach of charitable trust.

Then he played dumb in front of the jury when asked what he was being sued for — because the answer is everything he just confessed.

The bench reads behavior.

The contradiction is itself the record.

The witness already made the case for Musk.

kept.text:
🚨FYI: How did Greg Brockman's private diary end up as evidence in the Elon Musk vs OpenAI trial?

Brockman kept a personal journal on his laptop starting around 2010. It covered both his personal and professional life, including real-time notes from meetings and phone calls with Elon Musk, Sam Altman and Ilya Sutskever during OpenAI's earliest days.

The diary was uncovered during the discovery process of this lawsuit. Elon Musk's legal team obtained the entries and used them as key evidence. 

When Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers ruled in January 2026 that the case would go to trial, she specifically pointed to Brockman's diary entries as "ample evidence" supporting Elon Musk's claims.

OpenAI's lawyers have argued the entries are being taken out of context, calling them "staged for maximum misrepresentation." They say the quotes are cherry-picked from hundreds of pages of personal reflection that includes self-doubt and unguarded thinking, not a plan to commit fraud.

But Elon Musk's side sees it differently. Lines like "this is the only chance we have to get out from under Elon… take me to $1 billion" and "if three months later we're doing b-corp then it was a lie" are being read to the jury as proof that OpenAI's leadership always planned to move away from the nonprofit mission.

Brockman never expected anyone to read these entries. Now they are at the center of the most important tech trial in a generation.

quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: Greg Brockman's 2017 private diary entries read aloud in court reveal his true intentions behind the scenes at OpenAI.

"This is the only chance we have to get out from under Elon… take me to $1 billion."

"If three months later we're doing b-corp then it was a lie."

"I would actually be warm to steal the nonprofit from him to convert to b corp without him."

"His story will correctly be that we weren't honest with him in the end about still wanting to do for profit just without him."

Six days after telling Elon Musk he wanted to fundraise for the nonprofit, Brockman wrote: "We've been thinking that maybe we should just flip to a full profit. Making money for us sounds great and all."

kept.text:
Here's the part of the AI story nobody is saying out loud.

99% of Fortune 500 companies have adopted AI. Only 1% describe their rollout as mature.

The world's largest companies have access to every frontier model on earth.

And almost none of it has moved a single revenue number.

It's not a budget problem. It's not an intelligence problem. It's an installation problem.

Nobody inside these companies knows how to take a frontier model and turn it into work that actually changes the P&L.

McKinsey says 95% of GenAI pilots produce zero measurable impact. Deloitte says only 20% of enterprises have seen real revenue growth from AI.

Writer says 75% of execs admit their AI strategy is "more for show than guidance."

OpenAI and Anthropic both just bet billions on this exact gap.

OpenAI's deal with 19 PE firms including TPG, SoftBank, and Bain. Anthropic's $1.5 billion joint venture with Blackstone, Goldman, and Hellman & Friedman.

Same conclusion. Stop selling AI. Start installing it.

Here's what this means for you.

If you're a founder, you don't need to compete with OpenAI or Anthropic on models. You need to compete on installation.

The next big AI businesses won't be model labs.

They'll be the people who can walk into a Fortune 500 company and turn frontier AI into real revenue.

If you're inside a company, this is your fastest career move. The person on your team who learns how to deploy AI into actual workflows just became the most expensive person in the room.

If you're an operator, the gap is your edge. Most of your competitors have AI access. None of them have AI working. Whoever closes that gap first wins their category for the decade.

The next decade of AI won't be decided by who has the smartest model. It'll be decided by who closes the gap between "AI exists" and "AI runs my business."

This is the gold rush nobody is talking about yet.

kept.text:
Verifiable AI is Finally Here for Crypto
Most AI projects in crypto today are just fancy API wrappers. 

A smart contract pings OpenAI or similar, gets a result, and… hopes it’s correct. No proof it ran properly. No consistency. No real trust.

Ritual’s Infernet changes that by turning AI into a verifiable oracle.
The Problem (Current State)

•AI outputs have zero guarantees
•No proof of execution
•No consistency across runs
•No on-chain trust

How Infernet Fixes It

1Request — Smart contract defines the exact task (model + input + rules)
2Execute — Decentralized nodes run the same computation in parallel
3Verify — Nodes commit hashes first, then reveal results. The network checks for agreement
4Settle — Verified output lands on-chain. Honest nodes earn rewards, bad ones get slashed

Key shift: AI moves from “trust the black box” to provable, distributed compute.

What gets verified becomes real infrastructure  ready for DeFi agents, fair generative NFTs, autonomous protocols, and more.
Infernet makes decentralized intelligence something blockchains can actually rely on.
The future of on-chain AI isn’t just smarter. 

It’s verifiable.

أفضل 11 دورة مجانية في الذكاء الاصطناعي:

AWS — أساسيات هندسة الأوامر (Prompt Engineering)
تعلّم تقنيات تصميم الأوامر لاستخدام أدوات الذكاء الاصطناعي الخاصة بـ AWS.

Harvard — CS50: مقدمة في الذكاء الاصطناعي باستخدام بايثون
تعلّم أساسيات الذكاء الاصطناعي من خلال مشاريع تطبيقية باستخدام لغة بايثون.

Coursera — التوليد المعزّز بالاسترجاع (RAG)
شرح كيفية تصميم أنظمة RAG لتحسين دقة نماذج الذكاء الاصطناعي.

Microsoft — مقدمة في الذكاء الاصطناعي
يغطي المبادئ الأساسية للذكاء الاصطناعي، الشبكات العصبية، والتعلّم العميق.

Google — دورات أساسيات الذكاء الاصطناعي
تدريب عملي على استخدام أدوات الذكاء الاصطناعي في العمل اليومي والمشاريع.

OpenAI — موارد الأكاديمية
دروس تطبيقية على نماذج GPT، هندسة الأوامر، وتطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي.

Google — مقدمة في الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي
تعلّم كيف تقوم النماذج التوليدية بإنشاء النصوص، الصور، والأفكار.

OpenAI — دليل عملي لبناء الوكلاء (Agents)
يوضح كيفية تصميم ونشر وكلاء ذكاء اصطناعي لمهام واقعية.

Google — مقدمة في الذكاء الاصطناعي المسؤول
يغطي المبادئ الأساسية لبناء أنظمة ذكاء اصطناعي آمنة وأخلاقية.

Vanderbilt — هندسة الأوامر لـ ChatGPT
يشرح كيفية كتابة أوامر منظمة لإنتاج مخرجات واضحة وموثوقة.

Google Cloud × https://t.co/ut4uu6VRux — LLMOps
دليل لبناء ونشر وإدارة تطبيقات النماذج اللغوية الكبيرة (LLMs).

kept.text:
🚨 Uważaj! Cyberprzestępcy podszywają się pod twórców chatbotów AI!

🔎 Obserwujemy kampanię phishingową, w której oszuści wykorzystują wizerunek firmy OpenAI. Wysyłają e-maile o rzekomej konieczności ponowienia płatności za dostęp do ChatGPT, a zawarty w wiadomości link prowadzi do strony wyłudzającej dane kart płatniczych.

🔐 Jak się chronić?
🔹 sprawdzaj adres nadawcy i adres strony, do której przenosi link z wiadomości,
🔹 nie podawaj danych logowania ani karty płatniczej poza oficjalną stroną lub aplikacją. Pamiętaj, że zawsze możesz samodzielnie wyszukać właściwy adres w internecie,
🔹 w razie wątpliwości skontaktuj się bezpośrednio z dostawcą usługi.

⚠️ Zachowaj ostrożność kiedy otrzymujesz wiadomość, która wywołuje w Tobie emocje – np. straszy utratą dostępu. Cyberprzestępcy próbują w ten sposób wymusić pochopne, nieprzemyślane działanie, takie jak przekazanie im swoich danych lub pieniędzy.

👉 Pamiętaj, że podejrzane wiadomości możesz zgłosić do CERT Polska za pomocą formularza na stronie https://t.co/LghM5Rmujj lub w usłudze „Bezpiecznie w sieci” w aplikacji mObywatel.
✅ Pomagasz w ten sposób chronić siebie i innych!
